purpose: Reasoning-path crystal for the original problem pressure, target object, excluded routes, and core tensions that forced the project into its current form.

content_core.title: Problem Definition Crystal v1

nodes.label: ordinary RAG insufficiency
nodes.description: Flat recall and prompt stuffing do not provide durable state, credible update, conflict handling, rollback, or interpretable higher-order cognition.

nodes.label: memory is not just recall
nodes.description: A useful memory system must handle source, state, update, conflict, and provenance rather than just returning semantically similar text.

nodes.label: LLM is not replaced
nodes.description: The goal is not to build another base model or a total world simulator; the LLM remains responsible for generation and generalization.

nodes.label: external memory and governance layer
nodes.description: The project aims to build an external memory/cognitive-governance layer that complements Transformer-based LLMs.

nodes.label: retention vs abstraction
nodes.description: The system must preserve source material while still allowing higher-order structure and compression.

nodes.label: control vs emergence
nodes.description: The system must govern updates and retrieval without destroying discovery and growth.

nodes.label: usable memory over foundation-model replacement
nodes.description: The project prioritizes usable memory, credible updates, retrieval control, and evaluability over any attempt to become a substitute base model.

nodes.label: memory to experience to crystal to transfer
nodes.description: The problem matured from raw memory retention toward experience formation, crystal compression, and cross-agent transfer.

edges.description: Failures of flat retrieval force a broader definition of memory.
If memory includes source, state, update, and conflict, then an external governance layer becomes necessary.
The external layer must complement LLMs rather than attempt to replace them.
The system evolves because raw retention alone is insufficient but abstraction must remain grounded.
Governance is required precisely because uncontrolled emergence is risky, but hard control also damages usefulness.
Practical memory work keeps the project out of foundation-model replacement scope.

relation_paths: ordinary RAG insufficiency -> memory is not just recall -> external memory and governance layer
retention vs abstraction -> memory to experience to crystal to transfer
usable memory over foundation-model replacement -> LLM is not replaced -> external memory and governance layer

tensions.label: memory vs retrieval
tensions.description: The project is about memory as governed state, not just retrieval as access.

tensions.label: assistance vs autonomy
tensions.description: The system should support LLM cognition without trying to become an autonomous substitute intelligence.

tensions.label: grounded evidence vs higher-order compression
tensions.description: The project must move upward into abstraction without severing evidence links.

unresolved_conflicts.label: best governance thickness
unresolved_conflicts.description: How much governance is enough before the system becomes rigid remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: memory-shell role
unresolved_conflicts.description: Exactly how much structure should live in prompt/runtime vs graph/object layers remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: best regrowth interface
unresolved_conflicts.description: How much of the system should be expressed as transfer-ready crystal vs ordinary implementation remains unresolved.

source_anchors: this is not a new foundation-model route
LLM handles generation/generalization, memory system handles state/source/update/conflict/rollback
prioritize usable memory, controllable retrieval, credible updates, and evaluability
memory -> experience -> knowledge crystal -> Agent-to-Agent transfer
content_core.problem_space: What kind of memory/governance system is needed once flat retrieval is recognized as insufficient, but foundation-model replacement is explicitly out of scope.
core_questions: Why is ordinary retrieval insufficient for durable memory and cognition?
What exact responsibilities should remain in the LLM and what should move into external memory/governance?
Why did the project evolve from memory retention toward experience, crystal formation, and transfer?
scope_limits: This crystal defines the project problem and boundaries, not the final runtime protocol.
This crystal should preserve why the project exists, not how every module is implemented.
